name: D2L Brightspace API
what_it_does: Enterprise LMS REST API (Valence) for D2L Brightspace, covering courses, users, grades, content, discussions, and competencies across institutions serving 15M+ students.
use_cases: Automated grade passback from third-party assessment tools
Course creation and content deployment workflows
Enrollment and roster sync with SIS systems
Learning analytics extraction for institutional reporting
Competency and outcome tracking for accreditation workflows
not_for: Projects requiring real-time event streaming (polling required for most events)
Non-institutional developers without a Brightspace instance
High-frequency agent actions — rate limits are enforced per user/client
best_when: An agent orchestrates academic workflows for a university or K-12 institution already running D2L Brightspace, with institutional API credentials.
avoid_when: You don't have an institutional Brightspace deployment or need open-source/self-hosted LMS integration.

auth.notes: OAuth 2.0 with per-resource scopes. Supports authorization code flow and client credentials. LTI 1.3 integration available for tool providers. Scopes are registered per application in the Brightspace admin panel.

pricing.paid_tiers: Institutional licensing; pricing negotiated per contract
pricing.notes: Enterprise SaaS product — pricing is institutional and not publicly listed. No free developer sandbox available without an institutional contact.

performance.rate_limits: Per-client rate limits enforced; specific limits in institutional contract documentation

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Structured JSON error responses with HTTP status codes and descriptive messages. Better than Moodle but lacks the error detail depth of modern APIs like Stripe.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Read operations are safe and idempotent. Write operations lack idempotency keys; agents must implement their own deduplication logic.
known_agent_gotchas: FERPA compliance restricts automated access to student PII — agents must operate under institutional data policies
OAuth tokens expire and require refresh; agents must handle token lifecycle
API versioning uses date-based version strings (LP API 1.x) — must specify version in all requests
Webhook delivery is not guaranteed; agents should not rely solely on webhooks for critical state transitions
Institutional admin must whitelist your OAuth app before API access is granted
Some endpoints behave differently by institution based on enabled features/plugins

security_score_components.security_notes: OAuth 2.0 with scoped tokens is a significant improvement over token-based LMS APIs. FERPA and GDPR compliance built into the platform. SOC2 Type II certified. TLS enforced across all endpoints.
